text.title: Continuous Delivery Overview and Routing
text.summary: Router reference: Quick Reference definitions, CI vs CD vs Continuous Deployment matrix, DORA elite targets, deployment strategy summary, and routing table to child methodology files.

section.title: What is CD
section.p: Continuous Delivery is the practice of automatically preparing every passing commit for production release. The production deployment itself requires a manual trigger (Continuous Delivery) or fires automatically (Continuous Deployment).

list.item: Every commit is releasable: automated testing, feature flags, backward compatibility.
Deployment pipeline: automated path from commit to production-ready artifact.
Automate everything: build, test, deploy, rollback.
Feature flags: deploy code dark; release by toggling, not deploying.
Backward-compatible migrations: safe database changes across multiple deployments.

section.title: CI vs CD vs Continuous Deployment

rule.statement: Always quote this matrix when explaining the distinction; conflating CD (Delivery) with CD (Deployment) leads teams to enable auto-prod-deploy without the required safety nets.
rule.rationale: Teams commonly believe Continuous Delivery means auto-deploy to production; the matrix makes the manual-trigger distinction explicit.

section.p: Build automation: CI yes, CD yes, CDeployment yes. Automated tests: CI yes, CD yes, CDeployment yes. Deploy to staging: CI no, CD yes, CDeployment yes. Production-ready: CI no, CD yes, CDeployment yes. Auto-deploy to prod: CI no, CD NO (manual trigger), CDeployment YES (automatic).

list.item: Deployment frequency: on-demand, multiple per day.
Lead time for changes: under 1 hour from commit to production.
Blue-Green: two identical environments, instant cutover. Zero downtime, doubles infrastructure cost.
Canary: gradual traffic shift (10% then 50% then 100%). Limits blast radius; requires traffic routing.
Rolling: gradual pod replacement (default Kubernetes). Simple; brief mixed-version window.

rule.statement: Always route to the most specific child file; never answer implementation questions from this index alone.
rule.rationale: This index is a router; answering from it produces vague bullets that omit prerequisites, migration patterns, and concrete pipeline YAML.

list.item: CD principles, prerequisites, feature flags, expand-contract migrations, DORA targets, adoption roadmap: read cd-basics/.
Pipeline YAML, deployment strategies (blue-green/canary/rolling), health checks, monitoring, rollback: read cd-pipelines/.
Feature flag system design: read feature-flags/.
Branch strategy, trunk-based development: read trunk-based-dev-principles/ and trunk-based-dev-patterns/.
